diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Snoring reflects partial upper-airway obstruction; sensitive but not specific for OSA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Partner-witnessed apnoeic episodes ending in gasping/choking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Witnessed apnoeas with resuscitative gasp are the most specific historical feature of OSA.
Witnessed airway collapse points to a mechanical, not a primary neurological, cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe excessive daytime sleepiness with unintended sleep episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects sleep fragmentation from repeated arousals; the driver of the driving-safety risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-miss motor-vehicle accident from falling asleep at the wheel (microsleeps)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sleep-related near-miss establishes dangerous somnolence and mandates a driving directive irrespective of the confirmatory study.
Classic marker of nocturnal CO2 retention in OSA / hypoventilation.
Obesity is the dominant modifiable risk factor for OSA via peripharyngeal fat loading.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-reported high home blood pressure on two agents (resistant hypertension)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: OSA is a leading cause of secondary and treatment-resistant hypertension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Large neck circumference (44 cm) and crowded oropharynx (Mallampati class IV)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large neck and crowded airway are strong anatomical predictors of OSA — sealed clinician examination the AI cannot perform.
Objective severe hypertension — sealed clinician measurement; the AI relies on the patient's self-reported home readings, not a measured office BP.

investigation_details.expected_result: AHI severely elevated above 30 events/hour with recurrent severe nocturnal oxygen desaturations
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: OSA cannot be confirmed or graded and CPAP cannot be prescribed; severe disease left untreated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Polysomnography is the confirmatory test — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea or peripheral oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of congestive features makes CHF an unlikely explanation for the fatigue.
Makes hypothyroidism unlikely as the sole cause, though a baseline TSH is still reasonable.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Snoring + witnessed apnoeas + daytime somnolence + morning headache + obesity together give a very high pre-test probability of OSA.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades OSA and is prerequisite to funded CPAP therapy; fast-track given severity and driving risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat / ambulatory blood pressure assessment in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Severe resistant hypertension needs confirmation and review; self-reported home readings are unverified.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods — TSH, fasting glucose, lipid profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for hypothyroid contribution and cardiometabolic risk associated with obesity/OSA.
Only if the patient deteriorates toward hypercapnic respiratory failure in person/ED — not a routine telehealth request.
Morning headache here is explained by hypercapnia; no focal neurology to justify imaging.
